title: Refactor config resolution: 11 params → ConfigBuilder struct
description:
The resolve() function in bot/src/config.rs has 11 parameters, which:
- Forces every test to construct all 11 arguments (see line 142–145 boilerplate)
- Makes parameter order errors silent (caught only at runtime)
- Violates testability principle from /home/mark/.claude/CLAUDE.md

Fix: Move all CLI + env + file inputs into a ConfigBuilder struct that implements Default and has builder methods. This eliminates parameter order bugs and lets tests override only the fields they care about.

Example:
  let builder = ConfigBuilder::from_env()
    .with_relay(Some("ws://..."))
    .with_keyfile(None)
  let config = builder.resolve()?;

Acceptance: resolve() signature takes ≤3 parameters, test_missing_keyfile_uses_default() is 2 lines shorter, no functionality changes.
close_reason: deferred: 11-param resolve() is Phase 4f scope; ConfigBuilder is a Phase 4g refactor if the param count grows further
